page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 354.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.97 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16, which contribute to top-tier results in cryptographic operations, ray tracing, and software compilation. Memory subsystem performance is highly competitive, demonstrating top-tier read-only small block memory bandwidth and write large block bandwidth.

Given its high compute density and relatively low memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is optimized for compute-bound workloads rather than memory-intensive databases. It excels at parallel processing tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and large-scale text and image processing. Additionally, it demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high compute resource density, making it a viable option for highly parallelized CPU-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It delivers 180 dedicated vCPUs across 90 physical cores, paired with 354.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a lean 1.97 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory write bandwidth, and exceptional throughput in software compilation, cryptography, and static web serving. Supported instruction sets like AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16 accelerate specialized mathematical computations. This server is highly efficient for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, image processing, and LLM inference prompt processing, where high CPU density is required without the overhead of massive memory allocations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 180 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 354 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for highly parallelized, CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized server featuring 180 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 354 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server delivering exceptional multi-core processing power and memory bandwidth for parallelized CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 180 dedicated vCPUs and 90 physical cores.
Features 354 GB of RAM with a compute-focused 1.97 GB per core ratio.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory write bandwidth.
Supports AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16 instruction sets for accelerated cryptography and math.
Optimized for software compilation, static web serving, and LLM prompt processing.
